extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai won Stage 18 after sitting on GEOGHEGAN HART Tao's wheel up Laghi before counterattacking, taking pink by 12 seconds over teammate KELDERMAN Wilco.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan's 141-second advantage gained on the Stelvio descent before final climb reveals critical high-mountain domestique pacing threshold
GEOGHEGAN HART taking intermediate sprint bonus seconds while leading proves bonus hunting tactical value even when ultimately outkicked at finish
KELDERMAN struggling with rain jacket deployment on Stelvio summit cost rhythm that never recovered on 25km freezing descent
ALMEIDA lost 4:51 but not catastrophically collapsed—gradual distancing suggests sustainable threshold exceeded rather than complete implosion pattern

rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: sat on GEOGHEGAN HART entire Laghi climb then counterattacked to win, showing superior tactical patience and finishing speed
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: man-of-match pacing pulled 141 seconds on descent alone before final climb, elite high-altitude descending work capacity
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: took full descent to find rhythm after jacket issues, gained pink but by only 12 seconds over teammate, vulnerability exposed
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: led entire Laghi climb and tactically took 3-second bonus sprint, now only 15 seconds off pink despite being outkicked
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: lost 4:51 and pink after fortnight in lead, dropped halfway up Stelvio showing altitude accumulation fatigue
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: strongest from 15-rider break on Stelvio climb, breakaway climbing form signal
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: chased at one minute back and finished strong, found rhythm while leaders struggled, podium threat at small deficit
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: dropped with ALMEIDA halfway up Stelvio, no longer GC contender on high-altitude climbs
